name: Artichoke & Leek Pizza (Ew)
recipeIngredient: Salt and freshly ground black pepper, to taste
1 large leek, thoroughly cleaned and sliced thinly
2 oz mozzarella cheese, coarsely grated (2 1/3 cups)
recipeInstructions:
1. Place a pizza stone, baking tiles, or an inverted baking sheet on the lowest rack of a cold oven; preheat for 30 minutes to 500°F or the highest setting.
2. For each artichoke, peel away and discard tough outer leaves until you reach pale yellow leaves with darker green tops. Slice off the darkest green top and rub the artichoke with the cut side of a lemon half. Trim any remaining green portions. Rub with lemon. Remove the tough bottom 1/4 inch and pare away the tough outer skin. Use a melon baller or paring knife to remove the fuzzy choke from the center. Cut the heart into quarters, then into pieces.
3. Squeeze the artichoke pieces into a bowl of water mixed with lemon juice and place them in the acidulated water to prevent browning.
4. Heat a skillet over medium heat, add pancetta and cook, stirring until golden about 4 minutes. Drain on paper towels. Pour off fat from the skillet. Add 1 1/2 tsp olive oil and onions to the skillet. Cook, stirring occasionally, until onions are soft but not brown, about 3 minutes.
5. Add drained artichoke pieces and stir to mix well. Add pepper and 2 1/3 cups water. Cover and cook over low heat until artichoke pieces are tender and most of the liquid has cooked away, about 15 minutes. Add leeks and stir to mix well. Adjust seasonings with salt if desired.
6. Let cool completely.
7. Place dough on a lightly floured surface and pat into a disk. Roll or stretch the dough into a circle that is 1/4 inch thick and 10-12 inches in diameter. Transfer to a semolina or cornmeal dusted pizza peel or inverted baking sheet. Brush with a little remaining olive oil.
8. Distribute artichoke mixture over the dough. Sprinkle cheeses over the artichoke mixture. Drizzle with remaining olive oil.
9. Carefully slide the pizza onto the heated pizza stone and bake for 6-8 minutes, or until the bottom is crisp and browned and the top is bubbling.
